questions.question: What three questions trace back to nearly every bug in speech systems?
options: GPU model, batch size, and learning rate
What sample rate was the data recorded at vs what the model expects, is the signal aliased, and are you operating on raw samples or a frequency representation
Codec, container format, and file extension
Microphone brand, room acoustics, and cable quality
questions.explanation: Violating these conventions produces SILENT bugs: training runs fine but WER doubles, TTS ships a hiss, or a voice-cloning system memorizes the microphone instead of the speaker.

questions.question: What does the Nyquist-Shannon theorem say, and what happens above the Nyquist frequency?
options: Higher sample rates always sound worse

A sample rate sr can unambiguously represent frequencies up to sr/2; energy above that gets ALIASED — folded down into lower frequencies, corrupting the signal — so always low-pass filter before downsampling
Frequencies above sr/2 are simply silent
Nyquist only applies to analog signals
questions.explanation: This is why 8 kHz telephony audio (Nyquist at 4 kHz) kills consonants and should be avoided for ASR, while 16 kHz is the ASR standard consumed by Whisper, Parakeet, and SeamlessM4T.

questions.question: In a DFT of N samples at sample rate sr, what frequency does bin k represent?
options: k Hz exactly
k · sr / N Hz — magnitude gives the amplitude at that frequency, the complex angle gives the phase
sr / k Hz
k² / N Hz
questions.explanation: Example from the lesson: a 1024-sample FFT at 16 kHz gives 512 usable bins spanning 0-8 kHz at 15.6 Hz resolution. The FFT computes this in O(N log N).

questions.question: Why do we frame and window audio instead of FFT-ing the whole clip?
options: Whole-clip FFTs are illegal in most libraries
Speech changes over time, so we chop into overlapping ~25ms frames (10ms hop), multiply by a window (Hann/Hamming) to kill edge discontinuities, and FFT each frame — the Short-Time Fourier Transform
Frames compress the file size
GPUs can only process 1024 samples
questions.explanation: One FFT over a 10-second clip would tell you which frequencies occurred but not WHEN. The STFT preserves time-frequency structure — the foundation lesson 02's spectrograms build on.

questions.question: Which sample rates do modern speech systems standardize on?
options: Everything uses 44.1 kHz
16 kHz for ASR (Whisper, Parakeet), 24 kHz for modern TTS (Kokoro, F5-TTS, xTTS v2), 48 kHz for high-fidelity TTS and pro audio — with 16-bit PCM as the universal exchange format
8 kHz for everything to save bandwidth
Sample rate doesn't matter to models
questions.explanation: Feeding a model audio at the wrong rate is the classic silent failure: the tensor shapes work, the model runs, and the output is garbage.
